Description: Risk increases with age; most cases diagnosed after 50
Inherited gene mutations increase lifetime risk to 45-72%
First-degree relative with breast cancer doubles the risk
Dense breasts make cancer harder to detect on mammogram
Longer lifetime exposure to estrogen and progesterone
Excess fat tissue produces estrogen, fueling hormone-receptor-positive cancers
Risk increases 7-10% per standard drink per day
Regular exercise lowers estrogen levels and strengthens immune system
Combined estrogen-progestin HRT for >5 years increases risk
Radiation therapy to chest before age 30 significantly raises risk
Never having children or first child after 30 increases lifetime risk
